summary: The review of "MTA Example 2.pdf" indicates a generally low-risk posture, with most flagged clauses falling outside the scope of the University of Auckland's Preferred Contracting Positions or Standard Templates. However, several amber and one red flag highlight areas requiring attention. Key issues include intellectual property provisions that necessitate referral to Auckland UniServices Ltd for compliance, and a termination clause allowing for convenience, which conflicts with UoA's policy and requires escalation. The next step is to escalate the flagged clauses to the appropriate stakeholders, including UniServices and the Senior Manager for Research Grants and Contracts, to ensure alignment with institutional policies and risk tolerance.

flags.clause_title: 13. This Agreement will terminate on the earliest of the following dates: (a) o
flags.snippet: 13. This Agreement will terminate on the earliest of the following dates: (a) on completion of the Recipient’s current research with the Material, or (b) on thirty (30) days written notice by either party to the other, …
flags.rationale: The clause introduces termination for convenience (30 days written notice by either party), which is not acceptable under UoA Position #POS-19 for Material Transfer Agreements. Preferred termination terms require mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, with accrued rights unaffected and termination costs covered by the client. Termination for convenience is only acceptable for Consulting contracts under specific conditions, which do not apply here. Escalation to Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts is required.
flags.text: 13. This Agreement will terminate on the earliest of the following dates: (a) on completion of the Recipient’s current research with the Material, or (b) on thirty (30) days written notice by either party to the other, or (c) on the date specified in Exhibit B, provided that:

flags.clause_title: 1. The Provider retains ownership of the Material, including any Material conta
flags.snippet: 1. The Provider retains ownership of the Material, including any Material contained or incorporated in Modifications.
flags.rationale: This clause pertains to Intellectual Property ownership, specifically the Provider retaining ownership of the Material and its incorporation into Modifications. Intellectual Property clauses are governed by Auckland UniServices Ltd and must be referred to them for review. The clause does not conflict with UoA Preferred Contracting Positions but falls under the 'acceptable' tier for IP-related clauses, requiring referral to UniServices.
flags.text: 1. The Provider retains ownership of the Material, including any Material contained or incorporated in Modifications.

flags.clause_title: 6. The Recipient acknowledges that the Material is or may be the subject of a p
flags.snippet: 6. The Recipient acknowledges that the Material is or may be the subject of a patent application. Except as provided in this Agreement, no express or implied licenses or other rights are provided to the Recipient under any patents, patent applications, trade secrets or other proprietary rights of the Provider, including any altered forms of the Material made by the Provider. In particular, no express or implied licenses or other rights are provided to use the Material, Modifications, or any related patents of the Provider for Commercial Purposes.
flags.rationale: This clause pertains to intellectual property rights, specifically the acknowledgment of patent applications and restrictions on licenses for commercial purposes. Intellectual Property clauses are classified as AMBER under UoA Preferred Contracting Positions because they must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd for review. The clause does not materially deviate from standard IP practices but requires UniServices oversight.
flags.text:
6. The Recipient acknowledges that the Material is or may be the subject of a patent application. Except 
   as provided in this Agreement, no express or implied licenses or other rights are provided to the 
   Recipient under any patents, patent applications, trade secrets or other proprietary rights of the 
   Provider, including any altered forms of the Material made by the Provider. In particular, no express 
   or implied licenses or other rights are provided to use the Material, Modifications, or any related patents 
   of the Provider for Commercial Purposes.

flags.clause_title: 7. If the Recipient desires to use or license the Material or Modifications for
flags.snippet: 7. If the Recipient desires to use or license the Material or Modifications for Commercial Purposes, the Recipient agrees, in advance of such use, to negotiate in good faith with the Provider to establish the terms of a commercial license.
flags.rationale: This clause addresses the use or licensing of Materials or Modifications for commercial purposes, which involves intellectual property considerations. Intellectual Property clauses are always flagged as amber because UoA Positions defer IP matters to Auckland UniServices Ltd. The clause does not directly align with any UoA Preferred Contracting Position or Template wording, and must be referred to UniServices for review.
flags.text:
7. If the Recipient desires to use or license the Material or Modifications for Commercial Purposes, the 
   Recipient agrees, in advance of such use, to negotiate in good faith with the Provider to establish the 
   terms of a commercial license. It is understood by the Recipient that the Provider shall have no 
   obligation to grant such a license to the Recipient, and may grant exclusive or non-exclusive 
   commercial licenses to others, or sell or assign all or part of the rights in the Material to any third 
   party(ies), subject to any pre-existing rights held by others.

flags.clause_title: 8. The Recipient is free to file patent application(s) claiming inventions made
flags.snippet: 8. The Recipient is free to file patent application(s) claiming inventions made by the Recipient through the use of the Material but agrees to notify the Provider upon filing a patent application claiming Modifications 
flags.rationale: This clause pertains to Intellectual Property, specifically the filing of patent applications related to Modifications or methods of manufacture or use of the Material. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as UoA does not set IP positions independently. The clause matches the 'amber' tier due to the mandatory referral requirement.
flags.text:
8. The Recipient is free to file patent application(s) claiming inventions made by the Recipient through 
   the use of the Material but agrees to notify the Provider upon filing a patent application claiming 
   Modifications or method(s) of manufacture or use(s) of the Material.

flags.clause_title: 10. Except to the extent prohibited by law, the Recipient assumes all liability
flags.snippet: 10. Except to the extent prohibited by law, the Recipient assumes all liability for damages which may arise from its use, storage or disposal of the Material. The Provider will not be liable to the Recipient for any loss, claim or demand made by the Recipient, or made against the Recipient by any other party, due to or arising from the use of the Material by the Recipient, except to the extent permitted by law when caused by the gross negligence or willful misconduct of the Provider.
flags.rationale: The clause assigns all liability to the Recipient for damages arising from its use, storage, or disposal of the Material, with an exception for damages caused by the Provider's gross negligence or willful misconduct. While this deviates from UoA's preferred position under POS-07, which limits liability to direct losses and caps total liability, the clause does not impose liability on UoA and aligns with the principle of limiting the Provider's liability. The absence of a monetary cap for the Recipient's liability is not explicitly addressed in the UoA Positions for this scenario, but it does not directly conflict with UoA's obligations. This clause is therefore categorized as acceptable but requires careful review to ensure alignment with UoA's risk tolerance.
flags.text: 10. Except to the extent prohibited by law, the Recipient assumes all liability for damages which may arise from its use, storage or disposal of the Material. The Provider will not be liable to the Recipient for any loss, claim or demand made by the Recipient, or made against the Recipient by any other party, due to or arising from the use of the Material by the Recipient, except to the extent permitted by law when caused by the gross negligence or willful misconduct of the Provider.

flags.clause_title: (c) Without written consent from the Provider, the Recipient and/or the Recipi
flags.snippet: (c) Without written consent from the Provider, the Recipient and/or the Recipient Scientist may NOT provide Modifications for Commercial Purposes. It is recognized by the Recipient that such Commercial Purposes may requ…
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, specifically restrictions on commercial use of Modifications and licensing rights. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as UoA does not set IP policy independently. This clause falls under the 'amber' tier because it requires referral to UniServices for compliance and alignment with UoA's IP framework.
flags.text:
(c) Without written consent from the Provider, the Recipient and/or the Recipient Scientist may NOT 
       provide Modifications for Commercial Purposes. It is recognized by the Recipient that such 
       Commercial Purposes may require a commercial license from the Provider and the Provider has no 
       obligation to grant a commercial license to its ownership interest in the Material incorporated in the 
       Modifications. Nothing in this paragraph, however, shall prevent the Recipient from granting 
       commercial licenses under the Recipient’s intellectual property rights claiming such Modifications, or 
       methods of their manufacture or their use.

flags.clause_title: (i) if termination should occur under 13(a) or (c) above, the Recipient will d
flags.snippet: (i) if termination should occur under 13(a) or (c) above, the Recipient will discontinue its use of the Material and will, upon direction of the Provider, return or destroy any remaining Material. The Recipient, at its discretion, will also either destroy the Modifications or remain bound by the terms of this agreement as they apply to Modifications;
flags.rationale: This clause addresses termination obligations, specifically the handling of Materials and Modifications upon termination. While it aligns with the principle of accrued rights and benefits not being affected (Preferred Position under POS-19), the discretion granted to the Recipient regarding Modifications introduces ambiguity. This deviation from the Preferred Position requires confirmation by the Contract Manager to ensure compliance with UoA's termination standards. Escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts may be necessary if ambiguity persists.
flags.text:
(i) if termination should occur under 13(a) or (c) above, the Recipient will discontinue its use of the Material and will, upon direction of the Provider, return or destroy any remaining Material. The Recipient, at its discretion, will also either destroy the Modifications or remain bound by the terms of this agreement as they apply to Modifications;

    and

flags.clause_title: (ii) in the event the Provider terminates this Agreement under 13(b) other tha
flags.snippet: (ii) in the event the Provider terminates this Agreement under 13(b) other than for breach of this Agreement or for cause such as an imminent health risk or patent infringement, the Provider will defer the effective date of termination for a period of up to one year, upon request from the Recipient, to permit completion of research in progress.
flags.rationale: This clause addresses termination provisions, which are sensitive under UoA Position #POS-19. While it provides a mechanism for deferring termination to allow research completion, it deviates from the Preferred Position of mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency. The clause does not explicitly address termination costs or uncancellable obligations, which are required under the Preferred Position. However, the deferral mechanism aligns with the principle of protecting ongoing research, making it acceptable with further review. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is recommended to confirm alignment with UoA's termination policies.
flags.text: (ii) in the event the Provider terminates this Agreement under 13(b) other than for breach of this Agreement or for cause such as an imminent health risk or patent infringement, the Provider will defer the effective date of termination for a period of up to one year, upon request from the Recipient, to permit completion of research in progress. Upon the effective date of termination, or if requested, the deferred effective date of termination, Recipient will discontinue its use of the Material and will, upon direction of the Provider, return or destroy any remaining Material. The Recipient, at its discretion, will also either destroy the Modifications or remain bound by the terms of this agreement as they apply to Modifications.

flags.clause_title: 4. The Recipient and the Recipient Scientist agree to refer to the Provider any
flags.snippet: 4. The Recipient and the Recipient Scientist agree to refer to the Provider any request for the Material from anyone other than those persons working under the Recipient Scientist’s direct supervision. To the extent supplies are available, the Provider or the Provider Scientist agrees to make the Material available, under an agreement having terms consistent with the terms of this Agreement, to other scientists (at least those at Nonprofit Organization(s)) who wish to replicate the Recipient Scientist’s research; provided that such other scientists reimburse the Provider for any costs relating to the preparation and distribution of the Material.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the referral of material requests and the Provider's obligation to make materials available to other scientists under consistent terms. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy and template references.
flags.text:
4. The Recipient and the Recipient Scientist agree to refer to the Provider any request for the Material 
   from anyone other than those persons working under the Recipient Scientist’s direct supervision. To 
   the extent supplies are available, the Provider or the Provider Scientist agrees to make the Material 
   available, under an agreement having terms consistent with the terms of this Agreement, to other 
   scientists (at least those at Nonprofit Organization(s)) who wish to replicate the Recipient Scientist’s 
   research; provided that such other scientists reimburse the Provider for any costs relating to the 
   preparation and distribution of the Material.

flags.clause_title: 6. **Material**: Original Material, Progeny, and Unmodified Derivatives. The Ma
flags.snippet: 6. **Material**: Original Material, Progeny, and Unmodified Derivatives. The Material shall not include: (a) Modifications, or (b) other substances created by the Recipient through the use of the Material which are not 
flags.rationale: The clause defines the scope of 'Material' and explicitly excludes Modifications and other substances created by the Recipient that do not fall under Progeny or Unmodified Derivatives. This topic is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements, making it outside the scope of pre-established policy or template guidance.
flags.text: 6. **Material**: Original Material, Progeny, and Unmodified Derivatives. The Material shall not include: (a) Modifications, or (b) other substances created by the Recipient through the use of the Material which are not Modifications, Progeny, or Unmodified Derivatives.

flags.clause_title: 7. **Progeny**: Unmodified descendant from the Material, such as virus from vir
flags.snippet: 7. **Progeny**: Unmodified descendant from the Material, such as virus from virus, cell from cell, or organism from organism.
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Progeny' as unmodified descendants of the Material, which is a standard definition in Material Transfer Agreements but is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy tiers.
flags.text: 7. **Progeny**: Unmodified descendant from the Material, such as virus from virus, cell from cell, or organism from organism.

flags.clause_title: 8. **Unmodified Derivatives**: Substances created by the Recipient which consti
flags.snippet: 8. **Unmodified Derivatives**: Substances created by the Recipient which constitute an unmodified functional subunit or product expressed by the Original Material. Some examples include: subclones of unmodified cell lin
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the definition and examples of 'Unmodified Derivatives,' which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and requires no escalation unless it conflicts with other clauses or policies.
flags.text: 8. **Unmodified Derivatives**: Substances created by the Recipient which constitute an unmodified functional subunit or product expressed by the Original Material. Some examples include: subclones of unmodified cell lines, purified or fractionated subsets of the Original Material, proteins expressed by DNA/RNA supplied by the Provider, or monoclonal antibodies secreted by a hybridoma cell line.

flags.clause_title: 9. **Modifications**: Substances created by the Recipient which contain/incorpo
flags.snippet: 9. **Modifications**: Substances created by the Recipient which contain/incorporate the Material.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses 'Modifications,' which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. No specific policy or template reference exists for this topic.
flags.text: 9. **Modifications**: Substances created by the Recipient which contain/incorporate the Material.

flags.clause_title: 10. **Commercial Purposes**: The sale, lease, license, or other transfer of the
flags.snippet:
10. **Commercial Purposes**: The sale, lease, license, or other transfer of the Material or Modifications to a for-profit organization. Commercial Purposes shall also include uses of the Material or Modifications by any
	organization, including Recipient, to perform contract research, to produce or manufacture products for general sale, or to conduct research activities that result in any sale, lease, license, or transfer of the Material or Modifications to a for-profit organization. However, industrially sponsored academic research shall not be considered a use of the Material or Modifications for Commercial Purposes per se, unless any of the above conditions of this definition are met.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses 'Commercial Purposes,' which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and requires independent review.
flags.text: 10. **Commercial Purposes**: The sale, lease, license, or other transfer of the Material or Modifications to a for-profit organization. Commercial Purposes shall also include uses of the Material or Modifications by any organization, including Recipient, to perform contract research, to produce or manufacture products for general sale, or to conduct research activities that result in any sale, lease, license, or transfer of the Material or Modifications to a for-profit organization. However, industrially sponsored academic research shall not be considered a use of the Material or Modifications for Commercial Purposes per se, unless any of the above conditions of this definition are met.

flags.clause_title: 11. **Nonprofit Organization(s)**: A university or other institution of higher
flags.snippet: 11. **Nonprofit Organization(s)**: A university or other institution of higher education or a not-for-profit organization officially recognized or qualified under the laws of the country in which it is organized or loca…
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Nonprofit Organization(s)' and does not directly address Governing Law and Jurisdiction or any other UoA Preferred Contracting Position. It is a standalone definitional clause that does not fall under any specific UoA Position or template guidance. The amber flag raised by the deterministic comparator is not applicable here.
flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Position or Template
flags.text:
11. **Nonprofit Organization(s)**: A university or other institution of higher education or a not-for-profit organization officially recognized or qualified under the laws of the country in which it is organized or located, or any nonprofit scientific or educational organization qualified under a federal, state or local jurisdiction’s nonprofit organization statute. As used herein, the term also includes national, state or local government agencies.  

---

flags.clause_title: 11. This Agreement shall not be interpreted to prevent or delay publication of
flags.snippet: 11. This Agreement shall not be interpreted to prevent or delay publication of research findings resulting from the use of the Material or the Modifications. The Recipient Scientist agrees to provide appropriate acknowledgment of the source of the Material in all publications.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA's Preferred Position on Publication (POS-12). It explicitly preserves the right to publish research findings and requires acknowledgment of the source of the Material, which is consistent with UoA's policy. There is no mention of unreasonable embargo periods or restrictions that would conflict with academic freedoms or student publication rights.
flags.text: 11. This Agreement shall not be interpreted to prevent or delay publication of research findings resulting from the use of the Material or the Modifications. The Recipient Scientist agrees to provide appropriate acknowledgment of the source of the Material in all publications.

flags.clause_title: 14. Paragraphs 6, 9, and 10 shall survive termination.
flags.snippet: 14. Paragraphs 6, 9, and 10 shall survive termination. Page 4 ```
flags.rationale: The clause specifies survival of certain paragraphs post-termination, which aligns with the UoA Preferred Position under POS-19 that accrued rights and benefits are not affected by termination. Survival clauses are standard practice to ensure obligations like confidentiality, liability, and intellectual property persist as needed. This clause does not conflict with UoA's termination policy and is consistent with the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements.
flags.text:
14. Paragraphs 6, 9, and 10 shall survive termination.

Page 4
```
